sections.paragraph:
Ναθάμ, ὁ indecl. (נָתָן) Nathan, son of David (2 Km 5:14; Zech 12:12 Ναθάν, as Lk 3:31 v.l.; Just., D. 118, 2.—In Jos., Ant. 7, 70 Νάθας [acc.-αν]); in the genealogy of Jesus Lk 3:31.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

acc. acc. = accusative

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 665
